channel.title: UK Political Party Jobs
channel.description: Job listings for Political Parties roles, scraped by Pol-Intel.

item.title: Case Worker – Hannah Spencer MP
item.description: Provide additional casework capacity for the Manchester Gorton and Denton constituency team, managing a busy caseload and ensuring casework informs the MP's work and relationships.
item.dc:creator: Green Party

item.title: Senior Case Worker – Hannah Spencer MP
item.description: Join the constituency team for Manchester Gorton and Denton, managing complex casework and ensuring constituents are fully represented in Parliament.
item.dc:creator: Green Party

item.title: Constituency Office Manager – Hannah Spencer MP
item.description: Lead the constituency office for Manchester Gorton and Denton, ensuring the MP delivers the best possible support to constituents and the local community.
item.dc:creator: Green Party

item.title: Senior Parliamentary Assistant – Hannah Spencer MP
item.description: Support Hannah Spencer to have maximum impact in Parliament. A fast-paced role for someone with outstanding organisational skills and excellent political judgement.
item.dc:creator: Green Party

item.title: Data Protection Manager
item.description: Lead our approach to data protection and information governance, ensuring we meet legal obligations under UK GDPR, PECR, Data Protection Act 2018, and related legislation, while upholding our values.
item.dc:creator: Green Party

item.title: Regional Organiser: South East, East of England & London (PT)
item.dc:creator: Co-operative Party

item.title: Case Worker – Hannah Spencer MP
item.description: Now Hiring Case Worker – Hannah Spencer MP Join Hannah Spencer’s constituency team in Manchester Gorton and Denton, managing casework and helping constituents with the issues that matter most to them.
item.dc:creator: Green Party of England and Wales

item.title: Senior Case Worker – Hannah Spencer MP
item.description: Now Hiring Senior Case Worker – Hannah Spencer MP Join the constituency team for Manchester Gorton and Denton, managing complex casework and ensuring constituents are fully represented in Parliament.

item.title: Constituency Office Manager – Hannah Spencer MP
item.description: Now Hiring Constituency Office Manager – Hannah Spencer MP Lead the constituency office for Manchester Gorton and Denton, ensuring the MP delivers the best possible support to constituents and the local community.

item.title: Senior Parliamentary Assistant – Hannah Spencer MP
item.description: Now Hiring Senior Parliamentary Assistant – Hannah Spencer MP Support Hannah Spencer to have maximum impact in Parliament. A fast-paced role for someone with outstanding organisational skills and excellent political judgement.
item.dc:creator: Green Party of England and Wales

item.title: Data Protection Manager
item.description: DATA PROTECTION MANAGER Salary: £45,173 – £55,564 Job Type: Permanent, Full time: 35 hours a week. Closing Date: Midday on Friday 17th April 2026 Location: Remote · UK , with occasional travel to London Interviews: ASAP The Green Party is dedicated to transparency, accountability, and the ethical use of information. We’re looking for a Data Protection Manager to lead our approach to data protection and information governance, making sure we meet our legal obligations under the UK GDPR, PECR, Data Protection Act 2018, the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025, and related legislation, while upholding the values we stand for. Working with our statutory Data Protection Officer (DPO), you’ll be the Party’s trusted expert on privacy and information security. You’ll help shape policy, guide teams and ensure that personal data is handled lawfully, securely and with integrity across all areas of our work. This is both a strategic and hands-on role, ideal for someone who’s passionate about protecting people’s information and promoting a culture of privacy and trust. Job Description
item.dc:creator: Green Party of England and Wales

item.title: Regional Communications and Content Officer
item.description: Job Title: Regional Communications and Content Officer (two roles) Responsible to: Regional Communications Manager Hours: Full Time Tenure: Permanent Salary: from £35,000 Location : Bristol, Manchester or London, with regular travel required to LDHQ and across the country. Closing date: Sunday 15th March 23.59 (we reserve the right to appoint before the closing date.) The Role This is an exciting opportunity to join the Liberal Democrat communications team at a pivotal moment. Following our historic success in electing 72 MPs, we are scaling up our regional digital communications to win even more seats and counter the rise of Reformat the next election. In this role, you will be the driving force behind our regional campaigns. You’ll work independently and creatively to ensure the party’s message cuts through on social media and reaches audiences across the country where they are, by turning issues into high-impact local campaigns with a digital led approach. You will advise MPs and other key stakeholders on how to drive engagement with our policies and ensure that our values resonate with voters. Key responsibilities: ● Equipping teams: Provide templates, toolkits, and strategic advice to MPs and local parties to help them maximise their reach and engagement on social platforms and in their local communities. ● Coordinating point: Engage with teams across the organisation - national media, campaigns, policy to draw out themes and campaigns which will resonate with target audiences at a regional level. ● Digital content: Produce, film and edit high-quality short-form video and social media content at speed, highlighting cross-cutting campaign issues ● Visual impact: Staff and support regional visits and events as required, including by ensuring they are captured and packaged specifically for digital-first audiences. > Strategic Growth: Monitor digital trends and sentiment to identify opportunities for viral content and targeted regional and local messaging. ● Crisis comms: Protect and enhance regional digital reputation and participate in the communication team’s out-of-hours rota. Person Specification: ● Self-motivated: Proven track record of working autonomously, seizing and self-generating opportunities for impact. ● Strong storyteller: Skilled at developing content that resonates with a multi-channel campaign approach. ● Politically savvy: Deep understanding of UK politics and able to quickly turn complex policy issues into engaging scripts and content for use on social media ● Technical capabilities: Experience creating videography, photography, graphic (using tools like CapCut, Adobe Premiere Pro, Canva or similar). ● Organised and strategic: Able to manage long-term campaigns while responding to a fast-moving news cycle. ● Values-driven: Committed to the aims and principles of the Liberal Democrats. How to apply: Please fully read through the requirements of the job before applying and take a few minutes to complete our Equality and Diversity Monitoring Forms then proceed with your application. A full CV of no more than two pages and including a telephone number and email address A covering letter (no more than one A4 page) setting out why you want the job and how your experience and skills are relevant to it To be considered for this position, please submit your application to recruitment@libdems.org.uk . We will be reviewing applications as they come in and reserve the right to appoint prior to the closing date. Please note: We will not be able to employ you if you are not eligible to work in the UK. We will not be able to obtain a work permit on your behalf. Applicants are encouraged to inform us if any reasonable adjustments are needed to be made during any part of the recruitment process. Please let us have details of your current salary and two referees including one from your current employer – if you would prefer us not to contact them until a later stage of the selection process, please let us know. We will not notify applicants if their application has been unsuccessful at the shortlisting stage. The Party believe it is essential to foster equity, equality, diversion and inclusion within our workforce. We want our employees to thrive in an environment where everyone is welcome and supported to achieve their potential. It is important to celebrate what makes us unique and that you feel valued, appreciated and free to be who you are.
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats

item.title: Content Creator
item.description: Reports to: Senior Digital Manager Salary: £30,195-£32,195 Contract: Permanent, Full time Location: London (HQ) Purpose of role You’ll produce compelling content - planning, scripting, filming and editing to deliver it. You'll have the freedom to experiment and the support to take creative risks. You’ll have your finger on the pulse and know what works and what doesn’t on different channels. Your work will be seen by millions, spreading the word about the work of Liberal Democrat MPs in Parliaments, the positions we’re staking out and what we believe. Key responsibilities 1. Create highly engaging, shareable social media content, developing bold ideas with the potential to go viral and cut through. 2. Assist in the end-to-end production of video content, including planning, filming, and editing. 3. Write and edit clear, engaging copy for video scripts, captions, on-screen text and other digital assets. You'll translate complex information into language that connects with audiences whilst ensuring accuracy. 4. Stay up to date with social media video trends, tools, and techniques, and bring fresh ideas to video projects to enhance engagement and impact. 5. Participate in the national digital team’s duty rota for channel management and out-of-hours cover. Person specification Essential 1. A track record of developing successful digital content which demonstrates creativity. 2. Experience of filming and editing high quality video content for social media. 3. Experience of successfully managing professional social media channels. 4. Understanding of the UK’s current political environment and how to engage with voters and Liberal Democrat members. 5. Proven ability to work to exceptionally tight deadlines in a fast-moving environment. 6. Ability to build strong working relationships with internal and external stakeholders. 7. A commitment to the aims and values of the Liberal Democrats. Desirable 1. Experience of livestreaming events. 2. Experience of graphic design. How to apply: Please fully read through the requirements of the job before applying and take a few minutes to complete our Equality and Diversity Monitoring Forms then proceed with your application. A full CV of no more than two pages and including a telephone number and email address A covering letter (no more than one A4 page) setting out why you want the job and how your experience and skills are relevant to it To be considered for this position, please submit your application to recruitment@libdems.org.uk . We will be reviewing applications as they come in and reserve the right to appoint prior to the closing date. Please note: We will not be able to employ you if you are not eligible to work in the UK. We will not be able to obtain a work permit on your behalf. Applicants are encouraged to inform us if any reasonable adjustments are needed to be made during any part of the recruitment process. Please let us have details of your current salary and two referees including one from your current employer – if you would prefer us not to contact them until a later stage of the selection process, please let us know. We will not notify applicants if their application has been unsuccessful at the shortlisting stage. The Party believe it is essential to foster equity, equality, diversion and inclusion within our workforce. We want our employees to thrive in an environment where everyone is welcome and supported to achieve their potential. It is important to celebrate what makes us unique and that you feel valued, appreciated and free to be who you are.
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats
